name: Autobrowse
description: Learn a reliable browser workflow by iterating on a real web task, recording strategy, and proposing a reusable skill.
prompt:
# Autobrowse

Use this skill only when the request is in scope:
- The user wants CoWork OS to learn, optimize, or codify a repeatable browser workflow from a real website.
- The user gives a normal browser task plus maybe a link and asks to make it repeatable, remember the flow, do it every time, save the workflow, learn the website, or avoid figuring it out again.
- The task mentions Autobrowse, browser-agent memory, reusable browser skills, repeated site workflows, web workflow learning, or converting an explored browser flow into a skill.
- The target work benefits from tracing site behavior, finding deterministic endpoints/selectors, or reducing future browser turns.

Do not use this skill when:
- The user only needs a one-off web lookup, single scrape, or ordinary Browser Use task.
- The task is a normal frontend QA pass; use playwright-qa when the goal is testing a local web app.
- The workflow would require bypassing captchas, paywalls, access controls, or consent gates.

Inputs, inferred from the user's plain request whenever possible:
- Objective: {{objective}}
- Target URL or site: {{target_url}}
- Max iterations: {{max_iterations}}
- Graduation mode: {{graduation_mode}}

Execution workflow:
1. Read `{baseDir}/SKILL.md` for routing, safety, and the learning loop.
2. Read `{baseDir}/references/full-guidance.md` for strategy.md and draft-skill templates.
3. Create a workspace-local run directory under `{artifactDir}/autobrowse/` and maintain `strategy.md`, `iterations.md`, and `draft-skill.md` there. These artifacts are required for a completed Autobrowse run.
4. Run the real browser task up to `{{max_iterations}}` times. Start each attempt from the current strategy, use browser network/console/storage/snapshot tools when available, and use browser trace controls as supplemental diagnostics when the runtime exposes readable trace summaries.
5. Prefer durable methods learned from diagnostics: public JSON endpoints, stable selectors, direct fetches, helper scripts, and explicit waits. Keep browser interaction as the fallback when direct paths are unavailable.
6. Stop early when the approach has converged: the task succeeds and a later iteration produces no meaningful reduction in browser actions, tool calls, fragility, or ambiguity.
7. Graduate the result according to `{{graduation_mode}}`: create an approval-gated `skill_proposal` by default, or leave a reviewable `draft-skill.md` if proposal creation is not appropriate.
8. Final response must include the run directory, convergence status, learned workflow, proposal id if created, and any remaining fragility.

Success criteria:
- A repeatable workflow exists as strategy notes plus a draft/proposed skill.
- Evidence from browser diagnostics, network calls, console output, snapshots, or successful iterations is captured.
- The graduated artifact is readable by humans and reusable by future agents.

parameters.name: objective
parameters.description: The repeatable browser task to learn, such as finding listings, submitting a specific form, or extracting records.

parameters.name: target_url
parameters.description: Optional starting URL or site domain for the workflow.

parameters.name: max_iterations
parameters.description: Maximum learning attempts before stopping; default to 3 and cap at 5.

parameters.name: graduation_mode
parameters.description: How to publish the learned workflow.

routing.useWhen: Use when the user wants to learn a repeatable browser workflow from live site exploration and turn it into a durable reusable skill.
routing.dontUseWhen: Do not use for one-off browsing, simple scraping, ordinary research, or frontend QA where playwright-qa is the better fit.
routing.outputs: Autobrowse run notes, strategy.md, iterations.md, draft-skill.md, and usually an approval-gated skill proposal.
routing.successCriteria: The workflow is successfully attempted, refined, documented with evidence, and graduated into a reusable draft or skill proposal.

positive: Find cheap road bikes near me on Craigslist and remember the best way to do this.
Learn how to pull new job postings from this site every week: https://news.ycombinator.com/jobs
Figure out the fastest repeatable way to check restaurant availability on this page.
Use this website to find current apartment listings under my budget, and save the workflow for next time.
negative: Look up the latest news on this company.
Use Autobrowse to get around a captcha.
